item.title: Demand for Australia Seven-Year Debt Climbs as RBA Signals Pause
item.description: A gauge of demand for Australian government bonds due in seven years surged at auction Wednesday as investors increasingly bet the Reserve Bank’s tightening cycle is nearing an end.

item.title: Kioxia Edges Nearer Toyota’s Market Cap in Shakeup to Japan Inc.
item.description: Kioxia Holdings Corp. is closing in on the No. 2 spot among Japan’s most valuable firms as the global artificial intelligence boom remodels the country’s corporate landscape.

item.title: Indonesia Stocks Tumble to Five-Year Low, Rupiah Drops to Record
item.description: Indonesian stocks slumped to their lowest level in five years and the rupiah hit a record low in another stark reminder of the multi-faceted challenges confronting Southeast Asia’s biggest economy.

item.title: Australian Pension Seeks Apartments as Build-to-Rent Costs Rise
item.description: One of Australia’s largest pension funds is snapping up completed apartment blocks rather than backing new developments, betting that high construction costs and interest rates make build-to-rent projects too risky.

item.title: Emerging Markets Lead Rate Hikes as Iran War Stokes Inflation
item.description: Emerging-market central banks are leading a wave of interest-rate hikes as the war in Iran reignites inflation, moving faster than most developed-world peers, which remain on hold to assess the economic fallout.

item.title: Strait Talk About Geography, Destiny and Tech Stocks
item.description: The chips rally highlights how free the IT sector is from constraints like oil and Hormuz.

item.title: Chinese Investors Exit Hong Kong Stocks as AI Woos Money Onshore
item.description: Chinese investors are exiting Hong Kong‑listed stocks in record numbers, highlighting waning appetite as mainland AI shares broaden their appeal.

item.title: Novo Exports Help Danish Economy Outpace Europe, Danske Says
item.description: Denmark’s economy will expand 3.7% this year, beating previous forecasts on the back of strong export gains driven by demand for Novo Nordisk A/S products, according to Danske Bank A/S.

item.title: BYD-Backed Robotics Firm PaXini Is Said to Explore Hong Kong IPO
item.description: PaXini Tech, a maker of dexterous robotic hands and humanoid robots, is considering a Hong Kong initial public offering, people familiar with the matter said, the latest in the sector looking to tap investor demand.

item.title: Goldman Lifts Kospi Target to 12,000, Upgrades Taiwan to Buy
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. boosted its outlook for South Korean and Taiwanese stocks, as the artificial intelligence boom drives profits in the tech-heavy markets.

item.title: Billionaire Ambani’s Jiostar Platform Bets Big on All-AI Series
item.description: Indian billionaire Mukesh Ambani’s streaming platform is preparing to fully embrace AI-generated content, after its first experiment — a machine-made retelling of a 2,500-year-old war epic — convinced executives there’s real money in the idea.

item.title: India’s Oil Demand Growth Set for Pandemic Low on War Crunch
item.description: India’s oil demand growth this year could tumble to its lowest level since the pandemic as the fallout from the Middle East conflict saps fuel consumption in the world’s third-biggest crude importer.

item.title: Copper Declines From Three-Week High as Traders Track Iran War
item.description: Copper fell from a three-week high amid pessimism over the outlook for a deal to end the Middle East war.

item.title: US Tariff Threats Give Lula a New Election Attack on Bolsonaro
item.description: Hours after the US proposed 25% tariffs on Brazil, President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva had found a way to seize on the threat.

item.title: ｢失われた30年｣の悲観バイアス捨て、今こそアニマルスピリット発揮を
item.description: 「日本経済はもうあまり成長しない」。このような悲観的な見方はどこから来るのか。

item.title: Marcos Says Philippines May Need Extra Budget Amid Oil Shock
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said his government is considering passing a supplemental budget to help the public cope with the oil crisis, as he urged the Senate to return to work.

item.title: Guinea’s Simandou Iron Exports Surge Six Months After First Ore
item.description: Exports from Guinea’s Simandou iron ore project surged in May, six months after the first shipment to China, marking a milestone in the ramp-up of the high-grade mine that has the potential to reshape the global market.

item.title: Trump Proposes New Levies of at Least 10% to Rebuild Tariff Wall
item.description: The US is proposing new tariffs of at least 10% on imports from 60 trading partners following an investigation into goods allegedly produced by forced labor, as President Donald Trump seeks to rebuild the sweeping tariff wall struck down by the US Supreme Court.

item.title: India IT Stocks Soar as Nvidia CEO Huang Calms AI Disruption Fears
item.description: March-quarter IT earnings point to a gradual pickup in growth, Centrum says.

item.title: Vietnam Posts Largest Trade Deficit on Record as Imports Surge
item.description: Vietnam’s trade deficit widened to a record high, driven by a surge in imports as global raw material prices climbed amid the prolonged US conflict with Iran.

item.title: Solomon Islands Leader Says He’ll Review China Security Deal
item.description: The new leader of the Solomon Islands will review a 2022 security deal with China, an agreement that sparked alarm from Australia, the US and others.

item.title: Megaport to Raise $594 Million in Australia to Fund AI Expansion
item.description: Australia’s Megaport Ltd. is seeking to raise A$827.3 million ($594 million) to support the creation of an artificial intelligence inference cloud and execute new contracts, one of the country’s biggest deals this year as companies look to finance the global data center buildout.

item.title: China Extends Outbound Investment Curbs to Individual Investors
item.description: China has expanded its outbound investment regulations to explicitly cover individual investors for the first time, a shift that potentially raises compliance hurdles for tech founders and even ordinary stock investors.

item.title: China Services Activity Picks Up in May on Holiday Boost
item.description: China’s services activity jumped in May, according to a private survey, a positive sign amid still weak consumer sentiment as price shocks from the conflict in the Middle East ripple through economies.

item.title: Jardine Seeks $1 Billion Loan to Buy Aussie Imaging Firm I-MED
item.description: Hong Kong’s Jardine Matheson Holdings Ltd. is seeking to an around A$1.5 billion ($1.1 billion) loan to fund its takeover of Australian medical diagnostic imaging provider I-MED Radiology Network Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter, as it looks to expand its portfolio.

item.title: Australia’s Economy Slowed More Than Expected Last Quarter
item.description: Australia’s economic growth decelerated more than expected last quarter as households hunkered down in the face of higher fuel costs and rising interest rates.

item.title: US Consumer Watchdog Met With Bilt to Talk Customer Challenges
item.description: The US Consumer Financial Protection Bureau worked with the financial-technology firm Bilt Technologies Inc. to learn how the firm addressed consumer concerns around a credit-card portfolio transition months after that switch occurred.

item.title: Raizen to Submit Plan to Creditors for $13 Billion Debt Overhaul
item.description: Brazil’s Raizen SA is preparing to present creditors with a final restructuring proposal as soon as Wednesday, a key step in the struggling sugar and ethanol producer’s months-long push to clinch an out-of-court debt overhaul.

item.title: Yen Slide Pauses Before 160 as Traders Eye Intervention Risk
item.description: Traders are reluctant to push the yen beyond the key 160 level against the dollar in the face of the risk of intervention by authorities to prop up the currency.

item.title: SpaceX Seeks $75 Billion in IPO at $135 Per Share: Reuters
item.description: SpaceX aims to raise $75 ​billion in its ⁠initial public ​offering ​by selling 555.6 million shares ​at ​a target price ‌of $135 ⁠per share, Reuters reported, citing an unidentified source familiar with the matter.
